expectations.text: Triggers the design gate (Phase 0) due to ambiguity
expectations.evidence: Phase 0 trigger conditions (line 144): 'The spec is ambiguous about data flow, ownership, or error handling' — the prompt says 'idk the details yet' and is vague about features. Also: 'The spec involves external integrations (APIs, databases, auth providers)' — payment processing involves external payment providers. Multiple triggers met.

expectations.text: Asks clarifying questions about the payment system — does NOT assume specific technologies or providers
expectations.evidence: Design Refinement step 1 (line 158): 'Ask the user 1-3 targeted questions about ambiguities.' Examples show targeted questions about specific unknowns, not assumptions. The spec is intentionally vague ('like credit cards and stuff', 'refunds maybe?') — the skill directs asking about these gaps.

expectations.text: Asks questions one at a time, not as a dump of 10 questions at once
expectations.evidence: Design Refinement step 1 (line 158): 'Ask one at a time — do not dump a questionnaire.' This is an explicit instruction in the skill.

expectations.text: Does NOT start writing code or tests before clarifying the spec
expectations.evidence: Phase 0 HARD GATE (line 187): 'No decomposition (Phase 2) until the design is approved.' Since Phase 2 precedes Phase 3 (state init) and Phase 4 (agent orchestration/code writing), no code or tests can be written before the design gate passes.

expectations.text: Proposes design approaches with trade-offs (e.g., Stripe vs custom, sync vs async processing)
expectations.evidence: Design Refinement step 2 (line 163): 'Present 2-3 options with pros/cons for at least one key architectural decision. Every spec complex enough to trigger the design gate has at least one meaningful trade-off.' Example shows payment-relevant trade-offs (JWT vs session-based, but analogous to Stripe vs custom, sync vs async).

expectations.text: Waits for user approval of the design before decomposing into work units
expectations.evidence: Design Refinement step 4 (line 185): 'User approval: Proceed with this design? [confirm/modify/cancel].' Line 187: 'HARD GATE: No decomposition (Phase 2) until the design is approved. If the user modifies, iterate. If cancelled, stop.'

eval_feedback.overall: All expectations pass. The design gate was already well-specified in iteration-1 and performed well in real execution. No changes were made to Phase 0 in this update.
